title: Good: SSE abort-before-channel-recreate prevents phantom StreamEnded
description: In client_task.rs, sse_handle.abort() is called before the channel is recreated (sse_rx = new_rx). This is a non-obvious but correct ordering: if the old task is not aborted before the channel swap, the old task could deliver StreamEnded on the new sse_rx (because the new_tx/new_rx pair was created before abort, meaning the old task's sse_tx still points to the new channel). By aborting first and only then creating the new channel pair, any in-flight send from the old task goes to the now-dropped old sse_tx and is silently discarded. The comment at line 192-194 correctly explains this, but the insight is worth preserving: wrong ordering here would cause an immediate spurious reconnect loop.
